title: Drunken Boasts
description: Outrageous claims made by a tavern regular who has had one too many — for color, comedy, or the occasional grain of truth.

entries.result: I once punched a troll so hard it forgot what a bridge was.
The king owes me a favor. Three favors, if you count that thing with the goat.
I was there at the battle of the Copper Gates. Fifteen years old and I personally turned the tide.
I've been thrown out of every tavern in this city. This is the second-to-last one. I'm savoring it.
I once arm-wrestled a sea giant and it was a draw, which I count as a win because I'm still here.
There's a ballad about me. It's not entirely accurate, but the chorus is.
I've kissed a duchess. I won't say which one. She won't say either.
I taught the last headmaster of the academy everything they know about practical arcana. Don't look it up.
I found the lost tomb of the old emperors. I didn't take anything because I felt it was right to leave it undisturbed. I took one thing.
I once tamed a wyvern with nothing but a piece of dried meat and sheer force of personality.
My grandmother was a witch queen and I've got the cheekbones to prove it.
I can hold my breath for four minutes. Longer, if the thing I'm hiding from is slow.
I know where the Merchant Guild keeps its real ledgers. That's all I'll say about that.
I've walked the deep roads and I've got the map in here — taps head — it's not written down because written things get stolen.
I was offered the position of Royal Treasurer once. Turned it down. Too much honesty required.
I spent three months in the spirit world and came back voluntarily.
I can cook a meal that would make a war god weep. I've proven this, more than once.
A dragon once asked my advice. I gave it. The dragon was grateful. I will not describe the form the gratitude took.
There's a reason the old northern road is haunted, and I know what it is, and I am not proud of my involvement.
I once drank a wizard's entire stock of experimental spirits and walked home with only a slight shimmer.
I had a price on my head in four kingdoms simultaneously. Three of them have since been retracted. The fourth is old news.
I can read lips. I've been reading that table across the room for twenty minutes and you don't want to know what they're saying.
The royal family owes their throne to something my grandfather did. Not directly, but the chain of events is unambiguous.
I once beat a professional gambler at cards, dice, and arm wrestling in the same night. I didn't cheat. They did. I cheated better.
I know a word in the old tongue that, spoken correctly, can make anyone fall asleep. I will not demonstrate this in a public house.
I've swindled a tax collector. Not by lying — just by answering every question fully and accurately until they gave up.
My horse understands every word I say. Not in a mystical way. Just a very smart horse.
I've been in a dungeon — the proper underground kind — nine times. Left all nine. Under my own power each time, more or less.
I once bluffed my way through a border checkpoint using nothing but confidence and a letter of introduction I wrote myself.
I spent six months as a ghost. Not literally. But close enough that I've lost the right to correct people who say so.
I know the name of the thing that lives under this city. It's not a nice name, and it knows mine too.
I once won a game of riddles against a forest spirit and walked away without losing anything I needed.
I have a recipe for a cure that no healer in this town will give you. Seven ingredients. One of them is free.
A famous hero — I won't say which — learned the move that made them famous by watching me in a tavern brawl.
I've read the restricted section of the Grand Library. Every book. Three of them I wish I hadn't.
I once outran a centaur. Downhill, with a head start, but it counts.
I am owed a favor by the Lord of this city. He doesn't know it yet.
I've crossed the mountain pass in winter. Twice. The second time I knew better and it was still the hardest thing I've done.
I can tell the difference between a real enchantment and an imitation just by smell. Order me another drink and I'll demonstrate.
I've sewn up my own wounds on three occasions, twice in the dark.
I once told a fire giant it was wrong about something and I was right and it knew it and that is the whole story.
I've danced at a royal wedding. The couple didn't invite me. I attended anyway. They didn't ask me to leave.
I know where the old governor buried his treasure. I know because I helped bury it, which is complicated.
I was once a holy warrior. Technically still am, depending on how strictly you interpret the vows.
I've spoken directly with a god. A small one. It listened.
I once escaped from a locked room with nothing but a candle and a piece of string. This story ends exactly as well as it sounds.
I am the reason the old mages' quarter was rebuilt. Also the reason it needed to be rebuilt, technically.
I've traveled farther north than anyone in this room and several things I saw have no name in any language I know.
I taught a bear to open a gate. I am not explaining the context.
I've had three names. Only one of them has anything outstanding attached to it, and I'll not say which.
I survived a shipwreck by refusing to acknowledge it was happening until it had mostly finished.
I was declared legally dead once. Handled all the paperwork myself, which I thought showed initiative.
I know a fence in the dock quarter who can move anything. Anything. I won't say what I've tested this with.
I once convinced an ogre I was a tax inspector and collected a fee.
I have a scar that tells a story. Six stories, if you count correctly. They don't all agree.
I have defeated hunger, cold, thirst, and one very determined bailiff in the same week.
My uncle was one of the king's mages. I got the cleverness and he got the formal education. I still think I came out ahead.
I once talked down a mob by giving a better speech than the person who started it.
I know which herbs make a tracking dog lose a scent. Professionally speaking.
I've been to the underworld. Not all the way, but far enough to bring something back that I still haven't identified.
I spent a year and a half as a completely different person. Not metaphorically. The details are complicated.
I once negotiated a peace between two villages over a boundary dispute that had been running for sixty years. It held for four. That's longer than anyone expected.
The harbourmaster answers my letters faster than he answers the Lord's. Make of that what you will.
I've broken into a vault that the builders swore was unbreakable. They revised the claim. I don't hold it against them.
I once walked out of a battle carrying two unconscious companions and didn't notice until I got to the treeline.
I know seventeen ways to make someone trust you. I'm using two of them right now.
I once tracked a fugitive for sixty miles and lost them at the river. The river, not the fugitive, is what I cannot forgive.
I've held every position in a thieves' guild except leader, and only because I didn't want it.
I've been poisoned twice and cured myself both times because no physician was available and being inconveniently dead wasn't an option.
The greatest swordsman I ever faced wasn't human. I won by accident and I've been honest about that ever since.
I know where a portal opens at midnight on nights when the stars are right. Have not used it. Too busy. Thinking about it though.
I once convinced a cathedral to return stolen property by being politely persistent for an entire year.
I am on speaking terms with a ghost that has been haunting the old fort for a century. They're lonely and very boring.
I've read a contract and found eleven different ways it was unfavorable before signing it and negotiating every single one.
I once drank a creature's venom by accident and spent three days seeing things that may or may not have been real.
I know a prayer that actually works. Not because I'm pious. Because I worked out which words were functional.
I once bartered a knife into a horse over the course of a week by making a sequence of increasingly favorable trades.
I have lost three fingers to three different unrelated incidents. This is not a record.
I once found a map to something I'm not going to tell you about and decided not to go there, which is the smartest decision I've ever made.
There is a law in this city that was passed specifically because of something I did. It is a very specific law.
I can identify a noble's house by their servant's livery and by the way the servants carry themselves, which tells you everything.
I once wrestled a cursed knight out of a cursed armor and got thanked for it in seventeen languages.
I know more about the sewers under this city than the sewer workers. This is the kind of knowledge that comes with a smell.
I once escaped a prison by calmly walking out the front while the guards were arguing.
I survived three assassination attempts and I didn't even know about the third one until last year.
I know a song that drives rats out of a building. I know a second song that does the opposite. I have weaponized both.
I once spent a week as a different gender, entirely by accident, and came away with deep sympathy and a useful perspective.
I've slept in every type of bed from straw to silk. Straw is underrated and silk is overrated and I'll die on this hill.
I've walked across the desert and back. The desert is worse the second time because you know what's coming.
I was once turned into an animal and turned back. I won't say which animal. I will say it gave me perspective on a great many things.
I've talked my way past seven palace guards in seven different palaces in seven different languages.
I know the man who counterfeits the city seal. He owes me. Professionally speaking.
I once held a pass against an enemy army for six hours with twelve people and a lot of creative use of the terrain.
I have eaten things that would turn your hair white. Some of them were delicious. One of them definitely shouldn't exist.
I once had a complete conversation with a dying man that was entirely lies from both of us and we both knew it and it was the kindest exchange I've ever had.
I've stolen from the rich and given to the poor and stolen from the poor and given to the rich. Ethics is contextual.
I once talked a room full of armed men out of fighting by asking the right question at the right moment. Never managed it since.
The thing living in the well at the edge of town? I put it there. I also fed it for two years. It's been on its own since I left. Should probably check on it.
I know exactly where the bodies are buried. Literally, in three cases. Figuratively, in so many more.
I've done things that would make a saint weep and things that would make a devil uncomfortable. I sleep fine. The secret is never to think about it before bed.
